definitions.text: to continue to exist for longer than expected

collocation_evidence.example_text: The civil war lingered on well into the 1930s.

examples.text: The faint smell of her perfume lingered in the room.
The civil war lingered on well into the 1930s.

definitions.text: to stay somewhere for longer because you do not want to leave; to spend a long time doing something

examples.text: She lingered for a few minutes to talk to Nick.
We lingered over breakfast on the terrace.

definitions.text: to continue to look at somebody/something or think about something for longer than usual

examples.text: His eyes lingered on the diamond ring on her finger.

definitions.text: to stay alive but become weaker

examples.text: He lingered on for several months after the heart attack.

verb_forms.root: present simple I / you / we / they linger

definitions.text: a unit for measuring distance; 1 000 metres

examples.text: The industrial estate is 6 kilometres from the city centre.
She had been driving at 110 kilometres per hour.
Monaco is only two square kilometres in size.

definitions.text: to arrange things or people in a particular way or position

examples.text: The visitors disposed themselves in a circle round the statue.

definitions.text: to make somebody want to behave in a particular way

collocation_evidence.example_text: a drug that disposes the patient towards sleep
a drug that disposes the patient to sleep

examples.text: a drug that disposes the patient towards sleep
a drug that disposes the patient to sleep

verb_forms.root: present simple I / you / we / they dispose

definitions.text: far away from places where other people live

collocation_evidence.example_text: The site is remote from major population centres.

examples.text: a remote village/island/location/region
a remote community
a remote part of the country
a remote beach
one of the remotest areas of the world
The area is remote, mountainous, and roadless.
The site is remote from major population centres.
The farmhouse is remote from any other buildings.
in the remote past/future
a remote ancestor (= who lived a long time ago)
There are many facts about the remote past and the remote future that we cannot know.
a remote cousin

definitions.text: used to describe working away from a usual place, using communications technology

examples.text: Remote working has become the norm for many organizations.
Many people are working in remote or hybrid environments.

definitions.text: that you can connect to from far away, using an electronic link

examples.text: a remote terminal/database/server
His theories are somewhat remote from reality.
A novel should not be too remote from the experiences of its readers.
Jane felt remote from what was going on around her.

definitions.text: not very friendly or interested in other people

examples.text: When he spoke, his voice was cold and remote.
He somehow remains a remote figure.
There is still a remote chance that they will find her alive.
I don't have the remotest idea what you're talking about.
At that time, a new school building was just a remote dream.
a fairly remote possibility

phrases: a cry, gasp, squeal, etc. of delight

collocation_evidence.example_text: The children squealed with delight when they saw the puppy.
She won the game easily, to the delight of all her fans.
He takes (great) delight in (= enjoys) proving others wrong.
She took a simple delight in joys that we could all share.
She couldn't hide her delight at the news.
He expressed his delight at seeing us all again.

collocation_evidence.text: a cry, gasp, squeal, etc. of delight

examples.text: a feeling of sheer/pure delight
The children squealed with delight when they saw the puppy.
She won the game easily, to the delight of all her fans.
He takes (great) delight in (= enjoys) proving others wrong.
She took a simple delight in joys that we could all share.
She couldn't hide her delight at the news.
He expressed his delight at seeing us all again.

definitions.text: something that gives you great pleasure

collocation_evidence.example_text: the delights of living in the country

examples.text: This guitar is a delight to play.
Sample the culinary delights of Morocco.
the delights of living in the country
